reasoning: The control CCCS CM-7 focuses on ensuring least functionality, which in a Kubernetes context translates to restricting network access, limiting permissions, and enforcing strict security standards. NetworkPolicies can be used to ensure that ingress and egress traffic is denied by default, aligning with the principle of least functionality. RBAC can enforce least privilege by ensuring users and service accounts have only the permissions they need. Pod security standards can restrict pods from having excessive capabilities or running in privileged modes.
title: CCCS CM-7: Least Functionality
